response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack engineer with 15+ years of experience, but the profile is heavily weighted toward web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, React) rather than the modern C++ expertise this role demands. His only dedicated C++ experience was a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and he shows no exposure to industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), SDL practices, MQTT, or automation protocols. While his DLang low-latency and event-driven systems work demonstrates transferable systems-programming skills, the gap on the core requirement — hands-on senior-level C++ with STL, multithreading, and secure industrial development — is too large for a role expecting deep, current proficiency.
strengths: Proven high-performance, low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests/second
Broad engineering maturity: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership
Event-driven architecture experience via GISCollective and micro-services data integration
concerns: Minimal and outdated C++ experience (~6 months, ended 2015); C++ not even listed in current skills
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or OT-protocol exposure
Career trajectory is web/full-stack and AI tooling, diverging from embedded/industrial platform engineering

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or protocol experience
